# Per-metric derivations

*Generated from the methodology registry (`nextmillionai/methodology_spec.py`).*
This is the exhaustive reference for every number on the profile and report: the
exact code logic, our reasoning for it, and an honest **provenance** label. The
registry derives all numbers live from `scoring.py` and is guarded by
`tests/test_methodology_spec.py`; this doc is generated from it and checked in
CI, so the doc, the `/methodology` explorer, and the engine cannot disagree.

The narrative *why* is [`THESIS.md`](../../../THESIS.md); the bibliography
is [`docs/REFERENCES.md`](../../../docs/REFERENCES.md); the canonical contract is
[`SCORING-METHODOLOGY.md`](../SCORING-METHODOLOGY.md).


## Provenance — the honest class of every number

- **measured** — A direct count or ratio — true by definition of what was observed.
- **research-anchored** — The construct or its anchor traces to a cited study.
- **reasoned-default** — A sensible chosen constant — not externally validated, openly versioned, recalibrated as data accrues. An honest default, not a hidden assumption.
- **estimate** — An explicitly labeled band — never sold as an exact measurement.

A number's provenance is not a quality grade — it states what *kind* of claim it is, so the methodology is complete without overclaiming. *Reasoned-defaults* are chosen constants, openly versioned and listed in [Open questions](../SCORING-METHODOLOGY.md#open-questions); they recalibrate as data accrues. Meaning comes from construct validity, transparency, and this honest provenance — never from validation against a cohort, which the no-ranking, local-first design forbids.

## Quality dimensions

The composite is a mode-weighted average of these six. Each is scored
0–100 by mapping its sub-signals onto research-derived bands, then
averaging. Per-signal scaling anchors live in `scoring.py` (a reasoned
default flagged for hoisting + validation).

| Dimension | Weight | Provenance | Measures | Signals | Research |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Signal Clarity | 0.18 | research-anchored | The precision of your direction — how clearly you steer the AI. | first-shot acceptance rate, iterations to acceptance, reference-rich prompts, prompt specificity | METR (2025), PwC (2025), Forsgren et al. (2021) — SPACE |
| Build Stability | 0.22 | research-anchored | Whether the AI code holds up in your context — does it survive? | AI-line survival in commits, post-edit stability, error-fix rate | Veracode (2025), Nagappan & Ball (2005), Forsgren, Humble, Kim (2018) — Accelerate / DORA, Apiiro (2025) |
| Decision Weight | 0.18 | reasoned-default | The weight and durability of the decisions you make with AI. | decision impact, decisions that stick, plan-before-code signals | Forsgren et al. (2021) — SPACE |
| Recovery Velocity | 0.15 | research-anchored | How fast you recover when the AI gets it wrong. | debug-vs-generate ratio, error→fix convergence | METR (2025), Forsgren, Humble, Kim (2018) — Accelerate / DORA, Stack Overflow (2025) |
| Context Command | 0.12 | reasoned-default | Carrying context across tools and sessions without losing the thread. | reference & rules usage, MCP / context bridging (servers configured across Claude Code, Cursor & Claude Desktop, deduped — plus actual MCP tool-call usage), session continuity, cross-surface breadth | Forsgren et al. (2021) — SPACE |
| Orchestration Range | 0.15 | reasoned-default | The tools, models, and agents you coordinate to get work done. | tool count, MCP servers, max parallel agents, subagent dispatches, model routing | — |

## Composite & weighting

### Composite (strength index) — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Σ(dimension_score × mode-adapted weight) / Σ(weights of scored dimensions). Base weights live in scoring.py; they adapt to your dominant work mode via _MODE_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIERS (bounded 0.6–1.4), renormalized to 1.0. Unscored dimensions are excluded, never zero-filled.

**Why.** A single number must reflect excellence in HOW you build, not a generic ideal — so weights tilt toward the dimensions your mode depends on. The base weights and multipliers are reasoned defaults (an open question), bounded so no dimension is erased.

**Research.** Forsgren et al. (2021) — SPACE — Never reduce a developer to one number — measure across dimensions. Behind the six-dimension model and 'no single score predicts performance.'


## Archetypes & kinds

### Archetypes (crafts) — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Each of the 9 archetypes is scored independently: its sub-signals are each mapped to 0–100 by a linear/inverse band, then averaged equally. Agent-leverage signals only raise scores, never lower them. Levels: Elite ≥85, Advanced ≥70, Proficient ≥55, Developing ≥35, Emerging <35.

**Why.** Crafts are multi-hold and independent — you can be strong at several at once, never ranked on a ladder. Equal sub-signal averaging is a neutral prior: with no evidence one signal matters more, we don't pretend it does (an open question).

**Basis / caveat.** An archetype with no measurable signal is insufficient, not zero.

### Kinds (titles) — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Everyone with any measured AI activity holds the baseline kind (AI Explorer); specialized kinds are earned by crossing archetype thresholds: single-archetype kinds at ≥80 (Automation/CLI at ≥75); combination kinds need ≥70–80 across 2–3 archetypes; the legendary kind needs 6+ archetypes ≥75 with at least one ≥90. A specialized kind always outranks the baseline for the primary title.

**Why.** Kinds are crafts you hold, never rungs — and the entry craft is a real craft, not a void: a builder who has started but not specialized is an AI Explorer, not 'no kind'. The cutoffs (80/75/70) are reasoned thresholds; hard boundaries create a cliff at 79 vs 80 — a known limitation we'd smooth later.


## Work modes

### Work modes — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** 8 modes, each scored from weighted behavioral signals (e.g. Architect-First = 0.5·plan-ratio + 0.3·reference-usage + 0.2·composer-ratio). Dominant = highest; secondary = the others scoring above 20.

**Why.** Your dominant mode sets the composite's weighting, so it's measured from observable behavior, never self-reported. The per-signal weights (0.5/0.3/0.2) are reasoned primary/secondary/tertiary splits — chosen, not validated.


## Positioning (the map)

### Leverage mode (map Y) — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Three stages from agent-orchestration evidence: designs_the_loop when maxParallelAgents ≥ 3, OR agentModeRatio > 0.5 with maxParallel ≥ 2, OR ≥ 10 subagent dispatches across ≥ 3 sessions; harnessing when context scaffolding is present (CLAUDE.md / rules / MCP / hooks, or > 5 plans); prompting otherwise.

**Why.** Higher leverage is fit, not better — staying at prompting is correct for tightly-coupled work. The thresholds are reasoned defaults marking qualitative shifts in how much structure carries the agents.

**Basis / caveat.** Subagent/parallel evidence is Claude Code only.

### Build domain (map X) — indirect · measured

**How it is derived.** Per-repo classification by dependency markers, verified against import/call-sites when the --code scan runs (declared-but-unused SDKs don't count): ai_systems (agent frameworks: LangChain, CrewAI, MCP SDK…), ai_products (LLM SDKs: OpenAI, Anthropic…), else products. Primary = commit-weighted majority; the distribution carries the full footprint.

**Why.** What you build is a fact about your repos, not a judgment — read from real dependencies and (when scanned) actual usage, weighted by recent commits.

### Footprint cells — indirect · measured

**How it is derived.** Each repo placed in a (build-domain × leverage-stage) cell, weighted by its 6-month commit share; cells render at ≥ 1%.

**Why.** Shows where your real work sits across repos, not a single label — commit-weighted so active work dominates.


## Business fit

### Business fit (affinity) — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Per segment: round(Σ(min(your_archetype_score / required_min, 1.0) × weight × 100) / Σ weight). Strong fit = every minimum met. Top 3 surfaced. Report-only.

**Why.** Fit-to-segment, never builder-vs-builder. The per-segment archetype requirements and weights are reasoned editorial mappings of what each segment values — the softest part of the methodology, and the first we'd back with evidence.


## Your numbers

### AI code shipped — direct · measured

**How it is derived.** Sum of AI-attributed diff lines that survive in tracked commits (per-commit authorship attribution).

**Why.** Survival in commits — not raw generation volume — is the honest output signal; code that was reverted didn't ship.

**Basis / caveat.** Cursor scored commits only; ≥ 10 commits / ≥ 1000 lines, or shown insufficient.

**Research.** Nagappan & Ball (2005) — Code churn predicts defect density — behind the AI-line-survival framing.

### Max parallel agents — indirect · measured

**How it is derived.** Peak concurrent agents from overlapping subagent-run transcripts (hard evidence: their own timestamps), with within-tool session overlap as a softer floor. Cross-tool overlap never counts.

**Why.** Counts only genuine simultaneity from real timestamps — never assumed parallelism.

**Basis / caveat.** Claude Code subagent transcripts.

### Subagents dispatched — direct · measured

**How it is derived.** Count of Task-tool dispatches per session, accumulated in the local ledger.

**Why.** A direct count of agents you sent off to work — the clearest loop-design signal.

**Basis / caveat.** Claude Code only.

### Your session time — indirect · estimate

**How it is derived.** Gap-based active time where per-event timestamps exist (idle > 30 min excluded); first-to-last span capped at 8h otherwise. Ledger-preserved.

**Why.** Active, engaged time is the meaningful unit — not wall-clock — so idle gaps are removed where measurable. The 8h span cap and 30-min idle threshold are reasoned defaults grounded in deep-work / flow research.

**Basis / caveat.** True active time for Claude Code/Codex; open-session span (8h cap) for Cursor.

**Research.** Newport (2016) — Deep Work — Sustained, uninterrupted focus is the real unit of output — behind the deep/marathon session metrics and the gap-based active-time estimator.

**Research.** Csikszentmihalyi (1990) — Flow — Uninterrupted engaged stretches are the meaningful measure of working time — behind counting active time (idle >30 min excluded), not raw wall-clock.

### Agent runtime — indirect · estimate

**How it is derived.** Sum of subagent run spans (8h cap each) from their own transcripts, kept separate from your hands-on hours.

**Why.** Agents run in parallel, so their time can't be added to yours — it is tracked separately, never folded into your active hours.

**Basis / caveat.** Claude Code subagent transcripts only.

### Plan-first — direct · measured

**How it is derived.** Plan-before-code sessions / Claude Code sessions.

**Why.** Shown neutrally, never graded — planning is a mode, not a universal virtue.

**Basis / caveat.** Claude Code only (other tools don't expose plan mode).

### Avg prompt — direct · measured

**How it is derived.** Mean word count over parsed prompts.

**Why.** A signal of direction style, not quality.

**Basis / caveat.** Claude Code/Codex only; Cursor prompt bodies are never read.


## AI collaboration

### Surfaces split — indirect · measured

**How it is derived.** Per-tool share of your active days (days the tool appears / total active days). Git is the source, not a surface.

**Why.** Day-presence, not session count — so a heavily-logged tool doesn't dwarf one you use daily but lightly.

### Contribution heatmap — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Each active day shaded by the AI-authored share of that day's commits: ≥ 60% AI-heavy, ≥ 30% mixed, < 30% human-heavy; days without per-commit attribution show as activity-only (honest unknown).

**Why.** The 60/30 cutoffs are reasoned bands for mostly-AI vs mixed vs mostly-hand; we never guess a day's split when attribution is missing.

**Basis / caveat.** aiRatio is Cursor per-commit attribution.


## Lab (leverage & estimates)

### AI-authored share — direct · measured

**How it is derived.** AI-authored lines / (AI + human) lines over tracked commits.

**Why.** A counted authorship fact, not an estimate.

**Basis / caveat.** Cursor scored commits.

### Solo-equivalent time — indirect · estimate

**How it is derived.** A research-anchored BAND (≈ 1.33–2× your measured hands-on hours, from literature reporting ~25–50% task-time savings with AI — evidence is mixed). Lab-only, labeled an estimate, never a score input.

**Why.** We can't measure your counterfactual, so we never pretend to — it's an explicit band with mixed-evidence caveats, to be recalibrated per-user over time.

**Research.** METR (2025) — RCT, 16 developers, 246 tasks — 19% slower with AI despite predicting and perceiving a speedup. The operator, not the tool, drives outcomes.


## Confidence & integrity

### Confidence — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** round(completeness×25 + sources×20 + depth×30 + volume×15 + window×10), capped 95 (98 with the opt-in code scan). completeness = measured metric slots / 46; sources = collected / 4 standard; depth = fraction of scored dimensions on a sufficient sample (≥10 events); volume = active hours / 40; window = AI-era active days / 90.

**Why.** Confidence rewards SUBSTANCE, not raw counts — many short sessions over a long, sparse span no longer buys it. Depth is the biggest factor: when scores rest on small samples, confidence is held down even if breadth is high. The weights and the sufficiency threshold are reasoned defaults; the caps reserve the top for the deepest evidence.

### Trajectory — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Compares recent vs historical signal density, model adoption, and language breadth → accelerating / pivoting / declining / stable; insufficient under 14 days. Private by default.

**Why.** A you-vs-you trend, never against other people; the ratio thresholds (e.g. 1.3×) are reasoned defaults.

### Anti-patterns — indirect · reasoned-default

**How it is derived.** Risk flags from dimension combinations (e.g. high Signal Clarity with very low Build Stability), with prototyping modes excepted. Private by default.

**Why.** Surfaces genuine risk without penalizing a legitimate fast-prototype style; the trigger bands are reasoned defaults.

---
